comment_text: His tactical belt sounds like a Peterbilt's seatbelt clacking shut. His zippers sound like Lizzo riding down a zip line at 45 degrees. His apparently blunted knife being sheathed sounds like two aluminum yard sticks being scratched across each other. His NVGs sounds like an old CRT television being turned on. Pulling on his gloves tightly sounds like two balloons rubbing against each other. And his whisper on the comlink sounds more like a natural base trying to stage whisper loud enough for the nosebleed section to hear him. I've heard Batman whisper quieter than that. And yet, Sam Fisher is the most stealthy man in comics. I mean.. if you're a real fan, you'll watch it on mute.😂😋

video_title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell: Deathwatch | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
Ended up being a great watch, as much as i am weary of what some would say is DEI bs, this ended up being great. Albeit i haven't played splinter cell, and even i noticed the lack of a lot of stealth and a couple of times where the the female protag. shouldn't have been able to withstand as much as she did, the show and the story was still great.

I am not a fan of the amount of DEI being injected into preexisting content, but at some point you have to realize that even if everyone pushes back, some change is inevitable, and you just have to look for the light at the end of the tunnel, and find the few franchises where it makes sense and ends up being good.

Idk i thought it was good, stop whining every time you don't see a white man. but yeah, good show, good watch, needs wayyyy more stealth if they want to fit the "Splinter Cell" moniker they adopted for the premise. However, we need to understand that it won't be entirely stealth, because who wants to watch a character crouch behind a crate, shoot a light, and wait for 1-7 minutes for the guards to make the rounds and get into the precise positions needed to take advantage and stealthly get the objected and get tf out.

video_title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell: Deathwatch | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
The show is straight-up garbage.  

First off, zero respect for the source material.  

Second, Sam Fisher has *always* been portrayed as a traditional family man throughout the entire game franchise—no gay bestie nonsense. But of course, Netflix just couldn’t resist shoving their agenda down our throats and screwing it all up.  

Third, surprise surprise—the lead female agent is yet another “strong, independent” caricature with dreads. Don’t get me wrong, I’ve got nothing against Black operatives. The issue is she’s a walking woke checkbox, not a real character. And let’s be real—no field agent in their right mind would rock dreads on a mission. You keep your hair short so it doesn’t get in the way, and you *definitely* don’t yank off your tactical mask mid-op like some TikTok influencer.  

At this point, Netflix’s sheer cluelessness and creative bankruptcy have blasted past all known limits—and then some.

video_title: Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell: Deathwatch | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I went into this dark, expecting it to be good...
I start episode 1, excited and still shocked I even got a tv series of a golden game...
But then I see a Splinter agent.... Who is a woman?

Okay, that turned me off, but I continued, brushing it off for now...

Then I got introduced to my hero... Sam!!!!

But wait... He's an old man??

Then I literally knew where this was heading. I just knew the whole plot point and I won't lie, one of my greatest video game series which I've held close to my heart had crumbled.. just... Like ... That.

I mean.... I'm here sitting at home just simply astonished by what they've turned Splinter Cell into...

I'll just say this.... This is NOT Splinter Cell.

And additionally, whatever media comes out between 2024-2025-2026 plus even more maybe, I will never watch again, especially ones on Netflix.

I just finished Terminal List Dark Wolf, and this? F this. Literally F this.

I'm going to pretend like this doesn't exist and I'm okay with it.

0/10 from me.

comment_text:
Just keep this woke crap out of your game and actually target you audience you’ll make more money it’s tried and true 
But no It’s the same tired Netflix formula on repeat: buy a beloved IP, bait fans with nostalgia, then do the switch—sideline the male lead, bring in the “girl boss,” and call it fresh storytelling. We’ve seen this with Star Wars, Indiana Jones, Masters of the Universe, Assassin’s Creed, and now they’re lining up Splinter Cell for the same treatment.

The ratio says it all. Fans aren’t fooled anymore—we can spot the bait-and-switch a mile off. We didn’t ask for social engineering or activist tropes, we asked for Splinter Cell. Sam Fisher deserves better than to be wheeled out as a sidekick in his own series.

These companies forget that fans aren’t just viewers—we’re the lifeblood of these franchises. Keep ignoring us and all they’ll get is more backlash, more ratios, and more dead IPs
